trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "'The daylight's done, the nightmare's spun. It crawls off the loom, and into your room.' —Kithkin children's rhyme" The rhyme is attributed to the Kithkin of Lorwyn/Shadowmoor. The loom metaphor is apt: on Shadowmoor (Lorwyn's dark counterpart after the Great Aurora), Kithkin society was defined by the 'mindweft' — a shared empathic consciousness that in Lorwyn bound them in comforting harmony, but in Shadowmoor linked them in constant paranoia. A nightmare literally 'spun off the loom' of shared fear is cosmologically precise for this plane. [Scryfall: flavor_text; MTG Wiki: Kithkin, Lorwyn-Shadowmoor, via WebSearch snippet]

Kithkin are small humanoids native to the Lorwyn/Shadowmoor plane. Their villages, called douns in Shadowmoor, became armed fortresses against the plane's horrors after the Great Aurora transformed Lorwyn's idyllic landscape into a world of perpetual gloom. MH3 does not visit Lorwyn as a primary plane, but the Kithkin children's rhyme flavor text plants the card firmly in that plane's cultural register. The Horror type with the Kithkin-attributed rhyme creates a specific relationship: this creature is what Kithkin parents warn children about. [MTG Wiki: Kithkin / Lorwyn-Shadowmoor, via WebSearch snippet]

Horrid Shadowspinner is a 2/3 Horror for {1}{U}{B} with Lifelink and a loot-on-attack trigger (draw cards equal to power, discard that many). The combination of Lifelink and looting on an evasionless body is an unusual pairing — the Lifelink rewards attacking into stalled boards while the loot churns cards. EDHREC rank 11475 is among the lowest in the batch, suggesting limited Commander adoption. [Scryfall: oracle_text, edhrec_rank 11475]

Illustrated by Ben Wootten, a New Zealand-based artist with over 100 MTG credits including Birds of Paradise and multiple horror-adjacent commissions. The card was previewed by Ben Braun-Duin (source_uri confirmed in Scryfall preview data). Wootten's art here — abstract branching shadow-limbs against an ochre textured surface — departs from representational creature work toward horror abstraction. [Scryfall: artist = Ben Wootten, preview.source = Ben Braun-Duin; Gatherer artist search]
